97	class SamplingMetadata:
98	    """Metadata for input sequences. Used in sampler.
99	
100	    The usage is as follow;
101	    ```
102	    hidden_states = execute_model(...)
103	    logits = hidden_states[sampling_metadata.selected_token_indices]
104	    sample(logits)
105	
106	    def sample(logits):
107	        # Use categorized_sample_indices for sampling....
108	    ```
109	
110	    Args:
111	        seq_groups: List of batched sequence groups.
112	        selected_token_indices: (num_query_tokens_to_logprob). Indices to find
113	            logits from the initial model output hidden states.
114	        categorized_sample_indices: SamplingType -> token indices to sample.
115	            Each token indices is 2D tensor of (num_indices, num_indices) where
116	            the first item means the sample index within the returned logit
117	            (before pruning padding), and the second item means the sample
118	            index after pruning using selected_token_indices.
119	            For example, if the returned logit is [1, 2, 3], and we select
120	            [1, 2] for sampling, the pruned logit will be [2, 3]. In this case,
121	            The first tuple is [1, 2] (sampled index within original logit),
122	            and the second tuple is [0, 1] (sampled index within pruned logit).
123	        num_prompts: Number of prompt sequence groups in seq_groups.
124	        skip_sampler_cpu_output: Indicates if we want to skip the GPU=>CPU
125	            serialization of token outputs.
126	        reuse_sampling_tensors: Indicates if we want to reuse sampling
127	            tensors that are part of the sampler forward pass. Currently,
128	            it is mainly used for multi-step decode.
129	
130	    """
131	
132	    def __init__(
133	        self,
134	        seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
135	        selected_token_indices: torch.Tensor,
136	        categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, torch.Tensor],
137	        num_prompts: int,
138	        skip_sampler_cpu_output: bool = False,
139	        reuse_sampling_tensors: bool = False,
140	    ) -> None:
141	        self.seq_groups = seq_groups
142	        self.selected_token_indices = selected_token_indices
143	        self.categorized_sample_indices = categorized_sample_indices
144	        self.num_prompts = num_prompts
145	        self.skip_sampler_cpu_output = skip_sampler_cpu_output
146	        self.reuse_sampling_tensors = reuse_sampling_tensors
147	
148	    @staticmethod
149	    def prepare(
150	        seq_group_metadata_list: list[SequenceGroupMetadata],

148	    @staticmethod
149	    def prepare(
150	        seq_group_metadata_list: list[SequenceGroupMetadata],
151	        seq_lens: list[int],
152	        query_lens: list[int],
153	        device: str,
154	        pin_memory: bool,
155	        generators: Optional[dict[str, torch.Generator]] = None,
156	        cache: Optional[SamplingMetadataCache] = None,
157	    ) -> "SamplingMetadata":
158	        (
159	            seq_groups,
160	            selected_token_indices,
161	            categorized_sample_indices,
162	            num_prompts,
163	        ) = _prepare_seq_groups(seq_group_metadata_list, seq_lens, query_lens,
164	                                device, generators, cache)
165	        selected_token_indices = async_tensor_h2d(
166	            selected_token_indices,
167	            dtype=torch.long,
168	            target_device=device,
169	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
170	        )
171	        categorized_sample_indices = {
172	            t:
173	            async_tensor_h2d(
174	                seq_ids,
175	                dtype=torch.int,
176	                target_device=device,
177	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
178	            )
179	            for t, seq_ids in categorized_sample_indices.items()
180	        }
181	
182	        sampling_metadata = SamplingMetadata(
183	            seq_groups=seq_groups,
184	            selected_token_indices=selected_token_indices,
185	            categorized_sample_indices=categorized_sample_indices,
186	            num_prompts=num_prompts,
187	        )
188	        return sampling_metadata
189	
190	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
191	        return (
192	            "SamplingMetadata("
193	            f"seq_groups={self.seq_groups}, "
194	            f"selected_token_indices={self.selected_token_indices}, "
195	            f"categorized_sample_indices={self.categorized_sample_indices})")

198	def _prepare_seq_groups(
199	    seq_group_metadata_list: list[SequenceGroupMetadata],
200	    seq_lens: list[int],
201	    query_lens: list[int],
202	    device: str,
203	    generators: Optional[dict[str, torch.Generator]] = None,
204	    cache: Optional[SamplingMetadataCache] = None,
205	) -> tuple[
206	        list[SequenceGroupToSample],
207	        list[int],
208	        dict[SamplingType, list[int]],
209	        int,
210	]:
211	    """Prepare sequence groups and indices for sampling.
212	
213	    Args:
214	        seq_group_metadata_list: A list of sequence group to batch.
215	        seq_lens: A list of sequence lens per sequence group.
216	            Index of prompt len should match with seq_group_metadata_list.
217	        query_lens: A list of query lengths. Prompt lens include the length
218	            of entire prompt tokens, and it could be shorter.
219	        device: A device to use for random number generators,
220	            `SequenceGroupToSample.generator`.
221	        generators: A store of per-request random number generators used
222	            for seeded requests.
223	
224	    Returns:
225	        seq_groups: A list of sequence group to sample.
226	        selected_token_indices: See the definition from `SamplingMetadata`.
227	        categorized_sample_indices: See the definition from `SamplingMetadata`.
228	        num_prompts: Total number of prompts from `seq_group_metadata_list`.
229	    """
230	    # Batched sequence groups for the current model forward stsep.
231	    seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample] = []
232	    # A list of token indices to sample/compute logprob. It is used to
233	    # prune the outcome logits from the model for the performance.
234	    selected_token_indices: list[int] = []
235	    # Used for selected_token_indices.
236	    model_output_idx = 0
237	
238	    # Sampling type -> (
239	    # indices to sample/prompt logprob within pruned output logits,
240	    # indices to sample within pruned logits)
241	    categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, list[int]] = {
242	        t: []
243	        for t in SamplingType
244	    }
245	    # Index of logits to compute logprob. Logits include both prompt logprob
246	    # and sample logprob indices.
247	    logit_idx = 0
248	    # Total number of prompts from given sequence groups.
249	    num_prompts = 0
250	
251	    for i, seq_group_metadata in enumerate(seq_group_metadata_list):
252	        seq_ids = seq_group_metadata.seq_data.keys()
253	
254	        if cache is not None:
255	            sample_obj = cache.get_cached_seq_group_to_sample(len(seq_ids))
256	
257	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
258	                sample_obj.seq_ids[j] = seq_id
259	
260	            sample_obj.prompt_logprob_indices.clear()
261	            sample_obj.sample_indices.clear()
262	
263	        sampling_params = seq_group_metadata.sampling_params
264	        is_prompt = seq_group_metadata.is_prompt
265	        generator: Optional[torch.Generator] = None
266	        # If the current seq group is in decode stage, it is None.
267	        seq_len: Optional[int] = None
268	        query_len: Optional[int] = None
269	        prompt_logprob_indices: list[int] = (sample_obj.prompt_logprob_indices
270	                                             if cache is not None else [])
271	        sample_indices: list[int] = (sample_obj.sample_indices
272	                                     if cache is not None else [])
273	        do_sample = seq_group_metadata.do_sample
274	
275	        if seq_group_metadata.is_prompt:
276	            if sampling_params.seed is not None:
277	                generator = torch.Generator(device=device).manual_seed(
278	                    sampling_params.seed)
279	                if generators is not None:
280	                    generators[seq_group_metadata.request_id] = generator
281	
282	            num_prompts += 1
283	            num_prefill_sample = len(seq_ids)
284	            assert num_prefill_sample == 1
285	            assert query_lens is not None and seq_lens is not None
286	            query_len, seq_len = query_lens[i], seq_lens[i]
287	            # If we need sampling, exclude num_prefill_sample tokens from
288	            # prompt logprob.
289	            prompt_logprob_len = (query_len - num_prefill_sample
290	                                  if do_sample else query_len)
291	            sample_len = num_prefill_sample if do_sample else 0
292	        else:
293	            # Decode
294	            prompt_logprob_len = 0
295	            query_len = query_lens[i] if query_lens is not None and len(
296	                query_lens) > 0 else 1
297	            sample_len = len(seq_ids) * query_len if do_sample else 0
298	
299	            if sampling_params.seed is not None and generators is not None:
300	                generator = generators.get(seq_group_metadata.request_id)
301	
302	        # Update indices to select from the model output.
303	        """
304	        This blocks computes selected_token_indices which is used in the
305	        following way.
306	
307	        hidden_states = model(...)
308	        logits = hidden_states[selected_token_indices]
309	        """
310	
311	        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
312	            selected_token_indices.extend(
313	                range(model_output_idx, model_output_idx + prompt_logprob_len))
314	        model_output_idx += prompt_logprob_len
315	        if do_sample:
316	            selected_token_indices.extend(
317	                range(model_output_idx, model_output_idx + sample_len))
318	        model_output_idx += sample_len
319	
320	        # We now find indices for logprob computation and sampling.
321	        """
322	        This block computes categorized_sample_indices which is used in the
323	        following way.
324	
325	        hidden_states = model(...)
326	        logits = hidden_states[selected_token_indices]
327	        def sample(logits):
328	           # Use categorized_sample_indices for sampling.
329	           # prompt_logprob_indices to find prompt logprob indices.
330	           # sample_indices to find sample indices.
331	        """
332	
333	        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
334	            prompt_logprob_indices.extend(
335	                range(logit_idx, logit_idx + prompt_logprob_len))
336	            logit_idx += prompt_logprob_len
337	        if do_sample:
338	            sample_indices.extend(range(logit_idx, logit_idx + sample_len))
339	            categorized_sample_indices[sampling_params.sampling_type].extend(
340	                list(range(logit_idx, logit_idx + sample_len)))
341	            logit_idx += sample_len
342	
343	        if cache is not None:
344	            sample_obj.sampling_params = sampling_params
345	            sample_obj.seq_data = seq_group_metadata.seq_data
346	            sample_obj.seq_len = seq_len
347	            sample_obj.query_len = query_len

372	@dataclass
373	class SamplingTensors:
374	    """Tensors for sampling."""
375	
376	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
377	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
378	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
379	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
380	    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
381	    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
382	    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
383	    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
384	    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
385	
386	    @classmethod
387	    def from_sampling_metadata(
388	        cls,
389	        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
390	        vocab_size: int,
391	        device: torch.device,
392	        dtype: torch.dtype,
393	    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
394	        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
395	        output_tokens: list[array] = []
396	        top_ks: list[int] = []
397	        temperatures: list[float] = []
398	        top_ps: list[float] = []
399	        min_ps: list[float] = []
400	        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
401	        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
402	        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
403	        do_penalties = False
404	        do_top_p_top_k = False
405	        do_min_p = False
406	
407	        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
408	        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
409	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
410	            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
411	            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
412	            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
413	            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
414	            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
415	            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
416	            min_p = sampling_params.min_p
417	
418	            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
419	            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
420	            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
421	            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
422	                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
423	                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
424	                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
425	                temperature = 1.0
426	            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
427	                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
428	                do_top_p_top_k = True
429	            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
430	                do_min_p = True
431	            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS

Now let me create a comprehensive summary document with all the findings:

## COMPREHENSIVE ANALYSIS: vLLM Top-P and Top-K Filtering/Sampling Logic

Based on a thorough exploration of the vLLM codebase, here is a detailed breakdown of how top_p and top_k filtering logic is implemented:

---

### 1. PARAMETER DEFINITION (Starting Point)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397005761-lmj62x/vllm/sampling_params.py`

- **Lines 212-213:** `top_p` and `top_k` are defined as fields in the `SamplingParams` dataclass:
  ```python
  top_p: float = 1.0
  top_k: int = 0
  ```
- `top_p` controls cumulative probability of top tokens (0, 1] - default 1.0 (consider all)
- `top_k` controls number of top tokens to consider - default 0 (consider all), can be -1

---

### 2. DATA FLOW: SamplingParams → SamplingTensors

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397005761-lmj62x/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

#### 2.1 V0 (Legacy) Flow: `SamplingParams` → `SamplingTensors`

**Class:** `SamplingTensors` (Lines 372-597)

- **Method:** `from_sampling_metadata()` (Lines 386-495)
  - Extracts `top_p` and `top_k` from each sequence group's `sampling_params` (Lines 415, 419)
  - **Line 419:** `top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)` - Clamps top_k to vocab size
  - **Line 420:** `top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k` - Handles default values
  - Builds lists of top_p/top_k values, one per sample in batch (Lines 444-456)
  
- **Method:** `from_lists()` (Lines 498-597)
  - **Lines 545-550:** Converts `top_ps` list to torch tensor (float32, CPU, pinned memory)
  - **Lines 575-580:** Converts `top_ks` list to torch tensor (int32, CPU, pinned memory)
  - **Lines 585-587:** Moves tensors to GPU with non-blocking transfer:
    ```python
    top_ps=top_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
    top_ks=top_ks_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
    ```

**Result:** `SamplingTensors` object with:
- `top_ps: torch.Tensor` - shape (batch_size,), dtype float32
- `top_ks: torch.Tensor` - shape (batch_size,), dtype int32

---

### 3. ACTUAL SAMPLING/FILTERING LOGIC

There are **TWO main implementations** in vLLM:

#### 3.1 V1 (Modern) Implementation

**Primary File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397005761-lmj62x/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

**Class:** `TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module)` (Lines 22-118)

The class dynamically selects implementation based on hardware:

**Implementation A: PyTorch-Native (Lines 69-83)**
```python
def forward_native(self, logits, generators, k, p):
    logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)  # Filter logits
    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)  # Convert to probabilities
    return random_sample(probs, generators)  # Sample
```

**Implementation B: CUDA with FlashInfer (Lines 85-107)**
```python
def forward_cuda(self, logits, generators, k, p):
    if k is None and p is None:
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        return random_sample(probs, generators)
    return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
```

**Implementation C: TPU-Optimized (Lines 109-118)**
```python
def forward_tpu(self, logits, generators, k, p):
    logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
    return random_sample(probs, generators)
```

---

### 4. CORE FILTERING FUNCTIONS (V1)

**Function:** `apply_top_k_top_p()` (Lines 168-208)

**Key Characteristics:**
- **Input:** `logits` (batch_size, vocab_size), `k` (Optional[Tensor]), `p` (Optional[Tensor])
- **Output:** Modified logits with non-top tokens set to `-float("inf")`
- **Processing order:** Sort → Apply top-k → Apply top-p → Re-sort
- **Transformations:** Operates on **logits, NOT probabilities initially**

**Algorithm:**
```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p):
    # Sort logits in ascending order
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    if k is not None:
        # Apply top-k filtering
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    if p is not None:
        # Convert sorted logits to probabilities
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        # Compute cumulative sum
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        # Find tokens beyond cumulative probability threshold
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # Ensure at least one token
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Re-sort back to original vocabulary order
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**Special Case:** `apply_top_k_only()` (Lines 211-233)
- Optimized when only top-k is used (no sorting entire vocab needed)

**TPU Variant:** `apply_top_k_top_p_tpu()` (Lines 121-165)
- Avoids `torch.scatter` (slow on TPU)
- Uses cutoff-based approach instead

---

### 5. V1 INTEGRATION: Sampler Layer

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397005761-lmj62x/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py`

**Class:** `Sampler(nn.Module)` (Lines 18-227)

**Method:** `sample()` (Lines 90-137)
- **Line 121-126:** Calls `topk_topp_sampler` with metadata's top_k/top_p:
  ```python
  random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
      logits,
      sampling_metadata.generators,
      sampling_metadata.top_k,  # torch.Tensor, shape (batch_size,)
      sampling_metadata.top_p,  # torch.Tensor, shape (batch_size,)
  )
  ```

```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, p, k):
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    # Apply top-k
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Apply top-p
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Re-sort
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

---

When FlashInfer is available:

```python
def flashinfer_sample(logits, k, p, generators):
    if k is None:
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, p, deterministic=True)
    elif p is None:
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, k, deterministic=True)
    else:
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
```

**FlashInfer functions used:**
- `top_k_sampling_from_probs()` - Operates on probabilities
- `top_p_sampling_from_probs()` - Operates on probabilities
- `top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits()` - Operates on logits directly

```
         ↓
Sampler.forward()  or  Sampler.sample() (V1)
         ↓
    [Choose implementation based on hardware]
         ↓
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    │ PyTorch Native:                             │
    │ logits → apply_top_k_top_p() →              │
    │ set non-top tokens to -∞ → softmax →        │
    │ random_sample()                             │
    │                                             │
    │ FlashInfer CUDA:                            │
    │ logits/probs → flashinfer.sampling.*() →    │
    │ GPU-accelerated sampling                    │
    │                                             │
    │ TPU:                                        │
    │ logits → apply_top_k_top_p_tpu() →          │
    │ (cutoff-based, no scatter) → softmax →      │
    │ random_sample()                             │
    └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
         ↓
    Sampled token IDs
```

---

### 9. KEY TECHNICAL DETAILS

#### 9.1 Logits vs Probabilities
- **Filtering:** Done on **logits** (initially)
- **Top-p computation:** Requires **softmax(logits) → probabilities**
- **Final sampling:** From **probabilities**

#### 9.2 Data Types
- `top_p`: **float32** tensor (or double if specified)
- `top_k`: **int32** tensor
- `logits`: float32 or bfloat16

#### 9.3 Memory Optimization
- Tensors created on CPU with **pinned memory** first
- **Non-blocking async transfer** to GPU to hide transfer latency
- Can be **reused across decode steps** via `reuse_sampling_tensors` flag

#### 9.4 Masking Strategy
- Non-selected tokens set to `-float("inf")`
- After softmax: `-inf` becomes 0 probability (not sampled)
- At least one token always retained (`top_p_mask[:, -1] = False`)

#### 9.5 Batch Processing
- All samples in batch processed together
- Each sample gets its own `top_p` and `top_k` value
- Shape: `(batch_size,)` for parameters

### CONCLUSION

The actual top_p and top_k filtering/sampling logic is **NOT in a kernel**, but implemented in **pure PyTorch** with optional acceleration via:
1. **FlashInfer library** (GPU-accelerated, probabilistic sampling)
2. **PyTorch native operations** (sort, softmax, multinomial alternatives)
3. **TPU-optimized** algorithms (avoiding expensive `scatter` operations)

The flow is straightforward: parameters are extracted from `SamplingParams`, converted to batched tensors in `SamplingTensors`, passed to the sampler layer, and applied via masked filtering on logits before softmax and final sampling.

#### 3.1 V1 (Modern) Implementation

**Primary File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397005761-lmj62x/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

```python
    logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)  # Filter logits
    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)  # Convert to probabilities

```

**Implementation B: CUDA with FlashInfer (Lines 85-107)**

```python
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        return random_sample(probs, generators)
    return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
```

**Implementation C: TPU-Optimized (Lines 109-118)**

```python
    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
    return random_sample(probs, generators)
```

---

### 4. CORE FILTERING FUNCTIONS (V1)

**Function:** `apply_top_k_top_p()` (Lines 168-208)

**Key Characteristics:**
- **Input:** `logits` (batch_size, vocab_size), `k` (Optional[Tensor]), `p` (Optional[Tensor])
- **Output:** Modified logits with non-top tokens set to `-float("inf")`
- **Processing order:** Sort → Apply top-k → Apply top-p → Re-sort
- **Transformations:** Operates on **logits, NOT probabilities initially**

**Algorithm:**
```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p):
    # Sort logits in ascending order
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    if k is not None:
        # Apply top-k filtering
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    if p is not None:
        # Convert sorted logits to probabilities
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        # Compute cumulative sum
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        # Find tokens beyond cumulative probability threshold
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # Ensure at least one token
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Re-sort back to original vocabulary order
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**Special Case:** `apply_top_k_only()` (Lines 211-233)
- Optimized when only top-k is used (no sorting entire vocab needed)

**TPU Variant:** `apply_top_k_top_p_tpu()` (Lines 121-165)
- Avoids `torch.scatter` (slow on TPU)
- Uses cutoff-based approach instead

3	r"""Benchmark online serving throughput.
4	
5	On the server side, run one of the following commands:
6	    vLLM OpenAI API server
7	    vllm serve <your_model> \
8	        --swap-space 16 \
9	        --disable-log-requests
10	
11	On the client side, run:
12	    python benchmarks/benchmark_serving.py \
13	        --backend <backend> \
14	        --model <your_model> \
15	        --dataset-name sharegpt \
16	        --dataset-path <path to dataset> \
17	        --request-rate <request_rate> \ # By default <request_rate> is inf
18	        --num-prompts <num_prompts> # By default <num_prompts> is 1000
19	
20	    when using tgi backend, add
21	        --endpoint /generate_stream
22	    to the end of the command above.
23	"""

80	@dataclass
81	class BenchmarkMetrics:
82	    completed: int
83	    total_input: int
84	    total_output: int
85	    request_throughput: float
86	    request_goodput: float
87	    output_throughput: float
88	    total_token_throughput: float
89	    mean_ttft_ms: float
90	    median_ttft_ms: float
91	    std_ttft_ms: float
92	    percentiles_ttft_ms: list[tuple[float, float]]
93	    mean_tpot_ms: float
94	    median_tpot_ms: float
95	    std_tpot_ms: float
96	    percentiles_tpot_ms: list[tuple[float, float]]
97	    mean_itl_ms: float
98	    median_itl_ms: float
99	    std_itl_ms: float
100	    percentiles_itl_ms: list[tuple[float, float]]
101	    # E2EL stands for end-to-end latency per request.
102	    # It is the time taken on the client side from sending
103	    # a request to receiving a complete response.
104	    mean_e2el_ms: float
105	    median_e2el_ms: float
106	    std_e2el_ms: float
107	    percentiles_e2el_ms: list[tuple[float, float]]
108	
109	
110	def _get_current_request_rate(
111	    ramp_up_strategy: Optional[Literal["linear", "exponential"]],
112	    ramp_up_start_rps: Optional[int],
113	    ramp_up_end_rps: Optional[int],
114	    request_index: int,
115	    total_requests: int,
116	    request_rate: float,
117	) -> float:
118	    if (
119	        ramp_up_strategy
120	        and ramp_up_start_rps is not None
121	        and ramp_up_end_rps is not None
122	    ):
123	        progress = request_index / max(total_requests - 1, 1)
124	        if ramp_up_strategy == "linear":
125	            increase = (ramp_up_end_rps - ramp_up_start_rps) * progress
126	            return ramp_up_start_rps + increase
127	        elif ramp_up_strategy == "exponential":
128	            ratio = ramp_up_end_rps / ramp_up_start_rps
129	            return ramp_up_start_rps * (ratio**progress)
130	        else:
131	            raise ValueError(f"Unknown ramp-up strategy: {ramp_up_strategy}")
132	    return request_rate
133	
134	
135	async def get_request(
136	    input_requests: list[SampleRequest],
137	    request_rate: float,
138	    burstiness: float = 1.0,
139	    ramp_up_strategy: Optional[Literal["linear", "exponential"]] = None,
140	    ramp_up_start_rps: Optional[int] = None,
141	    ramp_up_end_rps: Optional[int] = None,
142	) -> AsyncGenerator[tuple[SampleRequest, float], None]:
143	    """
144	    Asynchronously generates requests at a specified rate
145	    with OPTIONAL burstiness and OPTIONAL ramp-up strategy.
146	
147	    Args:
148	        input_requests:
149	            A list of input requests, each represented as a SampleRequest.
150	        request_rate:
151	            The rate at which requests are generated (requests/s).
152	        burstiness (optional):
153	            The burstiness factor of the request generation.
154	            Only takes effect when request_rate is not inf.
155	            Default value is 1, which follows a Poisson process.
156	            Otherwise, the request intervals follow a gamma distribution.
157	            A lower burstiness value (0 < burstiness < 1) results
158	            in more bursty requests, while a higher burstiness value
159	            (burstiness > 1) results in a more uniform arrival of requests.
160	         ramp_up_strategy (optional):
161	            The ramp-up strategy. Can be "linear" or "exponential".
162	            If None, uses constant request rate (specified by request_rate).
163	        ramp_up_start_rps (optional):
164	            The starting request rate for ramp-up.
165	        ramp_up_end_rps (optional):
166	            The ending request rate for ramp-up.
167	    """
168	    assert burstiness > 0, (
169	        f"A positive burstiness factor is expected, but given {burstiness}."
170	    )
171	    # Convert to list to get length for ramp-up calculations
172	    if isinstance(input_requests, Iterable) and not isinstance(input_requests, list):
173	        input_requests = list(input_requests)
174	
175	    total_requests = len(input_requests)
176	    request_index = 0
177	
178	    for request in input_requests:
179	        current_request_rate = _get_current_request_rate(
180	            ramp_up_strategy,
181	            ramp_up_start_rps,
182	            ramp_up_end_rps,
183	            request_index,
184	            total_requests,
185	            request_rate,
186	        )
187	
188	        yield request, current_request_rate
189	
190	        request_index += 1
191	
192	        if current_request_rate == float("inf"):
193	            # If the request rate is infinity, then we don't need to wait.
194	            continue
195	
196	        theta = 1.0 / (current_request_rate * burstiness)
197	
198	        # Sample the request interval from the gamma distribution.
199	        # If burstiness is 1, it follows exponential distribution.
200	        interval = np.random.gamma(shape=burstiness, scale=theta)
201	        # The next request will be sent after the interval.
202	        await asyncio.sleep(interval)
203	
204	
205	def calculate_metrics(
206	    input_requests: list[SampleRequest],
207	    outputs: list[RequestFuncOutput],
208	    dur_s: float,
209	    tokenizer: PreTrainedTokenizerBase,
210	    selected_percentile_metrics: list[str],
211	    selected_percentiles: list[float],
212	    goodput_config_dict: dict[str, float],
213	) -> tuple[BenchmarkMetrics, list[int]]:
214	    actual_output_lens: list[int] = []
215	    total_input = 0
216	    completed = 0
217	    good_completed = 0
218	    itls: list[float] = []
219	    tpots: list[float] = []
220	    all_tpots: list[float] = []
221	    ttfts: list[float] = []
222	    e2els: list[float] = []
223	    for i in range(len(outputs)):
224	        if outputs[i].success:
225	            output_len = outputs[i].output_tokens
226	
227	            if not output_len:
228	                # We use the tokenizer to count the number of output tokens
229	                # for some serving backends instead of looking at
230	                # len(outputs[i].itl) since multiple output tokens may be
231	                # bundled together
232	                # Note : this may inflate the output token count slightly
233	                output_len = len(
234	                    tokenizer(
235	                        outputs[i].generated_text, add_special_tokens=False
236	                    ).input_ids
237	                )
238	            actual_output_lens.append(output_len)
239	            total_input += input_requests[i].prompt_len
240	            tpot = 0
241	            if output_len > 1:
242	                latency_minus_ttft = outputs[i].latency - outputs[i].ttft
243	                tpot = latency_minus_ttft / (output_len - 1)
244	                tpots.append(tpot)
245	            # Note: if output_len <= 1, we regard tpot as 0 for goodput
246	            all_tpots.append(tpot)
247	            itls += outputs[i].itl
248	            ttfts.append(outputs[i].ttft)
249	            e2els.append(outputs[i].latency)
250	            completed += 1
251	        else:
252	            actual_output_lens.append(0)
253	
254	    if goodput_config_dict:
255	        valid_metrics = []
256	        slo_values = []
257	
258	        if "ttft" in goodput_config_dict:
259	            valid_metrics.append(ttfts)
260	            slo_values.append(
261	                goodput_config_dict["ttft"] / MILLISECONDS_TO_SECONDS_CONVERSION
262	            )
263	        if "tpot" in goodput_config_dict:
264	            valid_metrics.append(all_tpots)
265	            slo_values.append(
266	                goodput_config_dict["tpot"] / MILLISECONDS_TO_SECONDS_CONVERSION
267	            )
268	        if "e2el" in goodput_config_dict:
269	            valid_metrics.append(e2els)
270	            slo_values.append(
271	                goodput_config_dict["e2el"] / MILLISECONDS_TO_SECONDS_CONVERSION
272	            )
273	
274	        for req_metric in zip(*valid_metrics):
275	            is_good_req = all([s >= r for s, r in zip(slo_values, req_metric)])
276	            if is_good_req:
277	                good_completed += 1
278	
279	    if completed == 0:
280	        warnings.warn(
281	            "All requests failed. This is likely due to a misconfiguration "
282	            "on the benchmark arguments.",
283	            stacklevel=2,
284	        )
285	    metrics = BenchmarkMetrics(
286	        completed=completed,
287	        total_input=total_input,
288	        total_output=sum(actual_output_lens),
289	        request_throughput=completed / dur_s,
290	        request_goodput=good_completed / dur_s,
291	        output_throughput=sum(actual_output_lens) / dur_s,
292	        total_token_throughput=(total_input + sum(actual_output_lens)) / dur_s,
293	        mean_ttft_ms=np.mean(ttfts or 0)
294	        * 1000,  # ttfts is empty if streaming is not supported by backend
295	        std_ttft_ms=np.std(ttfts or 0) * 1000,
296	        median_ttft_ms=np.median(ttfts or 0) * 1000,
297	        percentiles_ttft_ms=[
298	            (p, np.percentile(ttfts or 0, p) * 1000) for p in selected_percentiles
299	        ],
300	        mean_tpot_ms=np.mean(tpots or 0) * 1000,
301	        std_tpot_ms=np.std(tpots or 0) * 1000,
302	        median_tpot_ms=np.median(tpots or 0) * 1000,
303	        percentiles_tpot_ms=[
304	            (p, np.percentile(tpots or 0, p) * 1000) for p in selected_percentiles
305	        ],
306	        mean_itl_ms=np.mean(itls or 0) * 1000,
307	        std_itl_ms=np.std(itls or 0) * 1000,
308	        median_itl_ms=np.median(itls or 0) * 1000,
309	        percentiles_itl_ms=[
310	            (p, np.percentile(itls or 0, p) * 1000) for p in selected_percentiles
311	        ],
312	        mean_e2el_ms=np.mean(e2els or 0) * 1000,
313	        std_e2el_ms=np.std(e2els or 0) * 1000,
314	        median_e2el_ms=np.median(e2els or 0) * 1000,
315	        percentiles_e2el_ms=[
316	            (p, np.percentile(e2els or 0, p) * 1000) for p in selected_percentiles
317	        ],
318	    )
319	
320	    return metrics, actual_output_lens
321	
322	
323	async def benchmark(
324	    backend: str,
325	    api_url: str,
326	    base_url: str,
327	    model_id: str,
328	    model_name: str,
329	    tokenizer: PreTrainedTokenizerBase,
330	    input_requests: list[SampleRequest],
331	    logprobs: Optional[int],
332	    request_rate: float,
333	    burstiness: float,
334	    disable_tqdm: bool,
335	    profile: bool,
336	    selected_percentile_metrics: list[str],
337	    selected_percentiles: list[float],
338	    ignore_eos: bool,
339	    goodput_config_dict: dict[str, float],
340	    max_concurrency: Optional[int],
341	    lora_modules: Optional[Iterable[str]],
342	    extra_body: Optional[dict],
343	    ramp_up_strategy: Optional[Literal["linear", "exponential"]] = None,
344	    ramp_up_start_rps: Optional[int] = None,
345	    ramp_up_end_rps: Optional[int] = None,
346	):
347	    if backend in ASYNC_REQUEST_FUNCS:
348	        request_func = ASYNC_REQUEST_FUNCS[backend]
349	    else:
350	        raise ValueError(f"Unknown backend: {backend}")
351	
352	    print("Starting initial single prompt test run...")
353	    test_prompt, test_prompt_len, test_output_len, test_mm_content = (
354	        input_requests[0].prompt,
355	        input_requests[0].prompt_len,
356	        input_requests[0].expected_output_len,
357	        input_requests[0].multi_modal_data,
358	    )
359	
360	    assert test_mm_content is None or isinstance(test_mm_content, dict)
361	    test_input = RequestFuncInput(
362	        model=model_id,
363	        model_name=model_name,
364	        prompt=test_prompt,
365	        api_url=api_url,
366	        prompt_len=test_prompt_len,
367	        output_len=test_output_len,
368	        logprobs=logprobs,
369	        multi_modal_content=test_mm_content,
370	        ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
371	        extra_body=extra_body,
372	    )
373	
374	    test_output = await request_func(request_func_input=test_input)
375	    if not test_output.success:
376	        raise ValueError(
377	            "Initial test run failed - Please make sure benchmark arguments "
378	            f"are correctly specified. Error: {test_output.error}"
379	        )
380	    else:
381	        print("Initial test run completed. Starting main benchmark run...")
382	
383	    if lora_modules:
384	        # For each input request, choose a LoRA module at random.
385	        lora_modules = iter(

612	    print("=" * 50)

617	def check_goodput_args(args):
618	    # Check and parse goodput arguments
619	    goodput_config_dict = {}
620	    VALID_NAMES = ["ttft", "tpot", "e2el"]
621	    if args.goodput:
622	        goodput_config_dict = parse_goodput(args.goodput)
623	        for slo_name, slo_val in goodput_config_dict.items():
624	            if slo_name not in VALID_NAMES:
625	                raise ValueError(
626	                    f"Invalid metric name found, {slo_name}: {slo_val}. "
627	                    "The service level objective name should be one of "
628	                    f"{str(VALID_NAMES)}. "
629	                )
630	            if slo_val < 0:
631	                raise ValueError(
632	                    f"Invalid value found, {slo_name}: {slo_val}. "
633	                    "The service level objective value should be "
634	                    "non-negative."
635	                )
636	    return goodput_config_dict
637	
638	
639	def parse_goodput(slo_pairs):
640	    goodput_config_dict = {}
641	    try:
642	        for slo_pair in slo_pairs:
643	            slo_name, slo_val = slo_pair.split(":")
644	            goodput_config_dict[slo_name] = float(slo_val)
645	    except ValueError as err:
646	        raise argparse.ArgumentTypeError(
647	            "Invalid format found for service level objectives. "
648	            'Specify service level objectives for goodput as "KEY:VALUE" '
649	            "pairs, where the key is a metric name, and the value is a "
650	            "number in milliseconds."
651	        ) from err
652	    return goodput_config_dict

673	    # later if needed

675	    pt_records = convert_to_pytorch_benchmark_format(

686	        pt_file = f"{os.path.splitext(file_name)[0]}.pytorch.json"
687	        write_to_json(pt_file, pt_records)

## Comprehensive `min_p` Integration Analysis in vLLM

I've completed a thorough search across the entire vLLM codebase. Here's the complete integration pattern for `min_p`:

### Core Files with `min_p` Integration

#### 1. **vllm/sampling_params.py** - Definition & Validation
**Lines: 154-158 (docstring), 214 (field), 261 (constructor param), 304 (assignment), 381 (greedy reset), 426-428 (validation), 572 (str representation)**

- **Line 154-156**: Docstring defining min_p as "minimum probability for a token to be considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token. Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this."
- **Line 214**: Field declaration `min_p: float = 0.0`
- **Line 261**: Constructor parameter `min_p: float = 0.0`
- **Line 304**: Assignment in __init__ to self
- **Line 381**: Greedy sampling reset `self.min_p = 0.0`
- **Lines 426-428**: Validation `if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0: raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1]...")`
- **Line 572**: String representation includes min_p

#### 2. **vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py** - API Protocol Layer
**Lines: 262, 431-432, 474-476, 521, 767-770, 872, 922-924, 960, 1748-1751, 1772, 1795-1797, 1809**

- **Lines 259-262**: CompletionRequest field definition with `min_p: Optional[float] = None`
- **Line 431**: Default sampling param `"min_p": 0.0`
- **Lines 474-476**: Fallback logic for min_p in to_beam_search_params
- **Line 521**: Passed to SamplingParams
- **Lines 764-770**: ChatCompletionRequest field definition with docstring
- **Line 872**: Default sampling param `"min_p": 0.0`
- **Lines 922-924**: Fallback logic in to_beam_search_params
- **Line 960**: Passed to SamplingParams
- **Lines 1748-1751**: CompletionCreateParams field with docstring "Filters out tokens with a probability lower than `min_p`"
- **Line 1772**: Default sampling param `"min_p": 0.0`
- **Lines 1795-1797**: Fallback logic in to_sampling_params
- **Line 1809**: Passed to SamplingParams

#### 3. **vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py** - V0 Sampling Tensors
**Lines: 379, 399, 405-406, 416-417, 429-430, 446, 457, 485, 495, 503, 551-552, 588**

- **Line 379**: Tensor field `min_ps: torch.Tensor`
- **Line 399**: List buffer `min_ps: list[float] = []`
- **Line 405**: Flag `do_min_p = False`
- **Lines 416-417**: Extract from sampling_params `min_p = sampling_params.min_p`
- **Lines 429-430**: Check flag `if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS: do_min_p = True`
- **Line 446**: Prefill accumulation `min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len`
- **Line 457**: Sample accumulation `min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens`
- **Line 485**: Pass to from_lists
- **Line 495**: Return tuple includes `do_min_p`
- **Lines 503-504**: from_lists param `min_ps: list[float]`
- **Lines 551-552**: Create tensor `min_ps_t = torch.tensor(min_ps, ...)`
- **Line 588**: Move to device `min_ps=min_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True)`

#### 4. **vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py** - V0 Sampler Implementation
**Lines: 217, 223, 267, 288-289, 427-440**

- **Line 217**: Unpack return tuple from SamplingTensors `do_min_p = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(...)[3]`
- **Line 223**: Store as instance variable `self._do_min_p = do_min_p`
- **Line 267**: Retrieve in forward `do_min_p = self._do_min_p`
- **Lines 288-289**: Conditional application `if do_min_p: logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)`
- **Lines 427-440**: Implementation function:
  ```python
  def _apply_min_p(logits: torch.Tensor, min_p: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
      probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
      top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
      scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
      tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
      logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
      return logits
  ```

#### 5. **vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py** - V1 GPU Min-P Processor
**Lines: 227-306**

This is the V1 implementation with a dedicated LogitsProcessor class `MinPLogitsProcessor`:
- **Lines 232-242**: Data structures (cpu tensor, numpy view, device tensor, sliced view)
- **Lines 245-246**: `is_argmax_invariant()` returns True (min_p never impacts greedy)
- **Lines 248-249**: `get_min_p_by_index()` method
- **Lines 251-288**: `update_state()` method handling batch updates
- **Lines 290-306**: `apply()` method with broadcasting logic similar to V0
- **Lines 506-516**: Integration into LogitsProcessorManager with argmax_invariant handling

#### 6. **vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py** - TPU Metadata
**Lines: 10-12, 27-29, 103-115, 249-252, 255-256, 332, 389-390, 474, 566-567**

- **Lines 10-12**: Default sampling params includes `min_p=0.0`
- **Lines 27-29**: Tensor field `min_p: torch.Tensor = None`
- **Lines 103-115**: Data structures (min_p device tensor, cpu tensor, numpy view, request set)
- **Lines 249-252**: Update cpu data from sampling params
- **Lines 255-256**: Track requests with non-zero min_p
- **Line 332**: Remove from request set on removal
- **Lines 389-390**: Swap cpu values on move
- **Line 474**: Update when compacting batch
- **Lines 566-567**: Property `no_min_p` returns `len(self.min_p_reqs) == 0`

#### 7. **vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py** - TPU Sampler Implementation
**Lines: 62-64, 125-145**

- **Lines 62-64**: Application in forward:
  ```python
  if sampling_metadata.min_p is not None:
      logits = self.apply_min_p(logits, sampling_metadata.min_p)
  ```
- **Lines 125-145**: Implementation method with XLA-friendly operations:
  ```python
  def apply_min_p(self, logits: torch.Tensor, min_p: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
      probability_values = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
      max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
      adjusted_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
      valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_min_p
      logits.masked_fill_(~valid_token_mask, -float("inf"))
      return logits
  ```

#### 8. **vllm/v1/spec_decode/utils.py** - Spec Decode Utility
**Lines: 11-14**

Checks if spec decode should apply penalties:
```python
or sampling_params.min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS
```

#### 9. **vllm/config.py** - Configuration
**Line 1375**: Include min_p in available sampling parameters list

#### 10. **vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py** - V1 SamplingMetadata
**Note**: This file does NOT contain min_p fields. The V1 metadata uses LogitsProcessorManager instead.

### Test Files

#### 11. **tests/v1/sample/test_sampling_params_e2e.py** - E2E Tests
**Line 48**: Test with `min_p=0.5`

#### 12. **tests/v1/sample/test_logits_processors.py** - Logits Processor Tests
**Lines: 136, 234-236, 239-276, 405-407**

- **Line 136**: Initialize with `"min_p": 0.0`
- **Lines 234-236**: Test helper `_min_p_params()` function sets `kwargs["min_p"] = 0.1`
- **Lines 239-276**: Validation function `_min_p_validate()` checks masking behavior
- **Lines 405-407**: Test mapping for MinPLogitsProcessor

#### 13. **tests/v1/tpu/test_sampler.py** - TPU Sampler Tests
**Lines 34, 37, 52**: Multiple test cases with min_p values (0.2, 0.8)

#### 14. **tests/v1/tpu/test_perf.py** - TPU Performance Tests
**Line 113**: Perf test with `min_p=0.0`

#### 15. **tests/entrypoints/openai/test_transcription_validation.py** - API Tests
**Line 218**: Audio transcription test with `min_p=0.5` in extra_body

### Benchmark & Example Files

#### 16. **benchmarks/benchmark_serving.py** - Serving Benchmark
**Lines: 864-873**: Collection of sampling params includes min_p (line 869)

#### 17. **vllm/benchmarks/serve.py** - vLLM Benchmark
**Lines: 937-951**: Collection of sampling params includes min_p (line 944)

### Model-Specific Files (False Positives)

Files that mention `min_p` but for **image processing** (min_pixels/max_pixels), NOT sampling:
- vllm/model_executor/models/qwen2_vl.py
- vllm/model_executor/models/qwen2_5_vl.py
- vllm/model_executor/models/qwen2_5_omni_thinker.py
- vllm/model_executor/models/keye.py
- vllm/model_executor/models/aya_vision.py
- examples/offline_inference/vision_language.py
- tests/models/multimodal/processing/test_qwen2_vl.py
- tests/lora/test_qwen2vl.py

### Summary of Integration Points

**Complete flow for `top_a` (following min_p pattern):**

1. **API Definition** → `protocol.py` (CompletionRequest, ChatCompletionRequest, CompletionCreateParams)
2. **SamplingParams** → `sampling_params.py` (field, docstring, validation, greedy reset)
3. **V0 Path**: 
   - `sampling_metadata.py` → accumulate into SamplingTensors
   - `sampler.py` → apply via `_apply_min_p()` function
4. **V1 GPU Path**:
   - `logits_processor.py` → MinPLogitsProcessor class with batching logic
5. **V1 TPU Path**:
   - `tpu/metadata.py` → batching similar to GPU
   - `tpu/sampler.py` → apply with XLA-friendly ops
6. **Utility checks** → `spec_decode/utils.py` for penalty detection
7. **Configuration** → `config.py` for available parameters

This is the exact pattern you'll need to replicate for implementing `top_a`.

  ```python
  def apply_min_p(self, logits: torch.Tensor, min_p: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
      probability_values = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
      max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
      adjusted_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
      valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_min_p
      logits.masked_fill_(~valid_token_mask, -float("inf"))
      return logits
  ```

###### ----- Built-in LogitsProcessor impls below here


class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):

    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
                 device: DeviceLikeType):
        super().__init__()
        self.min_p_count: int = 0

        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
                                            dtype=torch.float32,
                                            device="cpu",
                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        # Pre-allocated device tensor
        self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
                                                      device=device)
        # Current slice of the device tensor
        self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]

    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
        """Min-p never impacts greedy sampling"""
        return True

    def get_min_p_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
        return float(self.min_p_cpu[index])

    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
        if not batch_update:
            return

        needs_update = False
        # Process added requests.
        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
            min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
            if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
                needs_update = True
                self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
            if min_p:
                self.min_p_count += 1

        if self.min_p_count:
            # Process removed requests.
            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
            for index in batch_update.removed:
                if self.min_p_cpu[index]:
                    self.min_p_count -= 1

            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
                change = (min_p_a :=
                          self.min_p_cpu[adx]) != (min_p_b :=
                                                   self.min_p_cpu[bdx])
                needs_update |= change
                if change:
                    self.min_p_cpu[bdx] = min_p_a
                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
                        self.min_p_cpu[adx] = min_p_b

        # Update tensors if needed.
        size = batch_update.batch_size
        if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
            self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
            self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
            self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)

    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if not self.min_p_count:
            return logits

        # Convert logits to probability distribution
        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
                                       dim=-1,
                                       keepdim=True)
        # Adjust min_p
        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
        return logits


class LogitBiasLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):

    def __init__(self, pin_memory: bool, device: torch.device):
        super().__init__()
        self.biases: dict[int, dict[int, float]] = {}
        self.device = device
        self.pin_memory = pin_memory

        self.bias_tensor: torch.Tensor = torch.tensor(())
        self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor([], torch.int32),
                             self._device_tensor([], torch.int32))

# Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,

def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))

    return logits

def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if self.min_toks:
            # Inhibit EOS token for requests which have not reached min length
            logits[self.logits_slice] = -float("inf")
        return logits


def init_builtin_logitsprocs(pin_memory_available: bool, max_num_reqs: int,
                             device: torch.device) -> LogitsProcessorManager:
    """Construct 'builtin' vLLM logitsprocs which the engine
    loads by default.

    Args:
      pin_memory_available: pinned memory is available for use
                            for use by logitsproc
      max_num_reqs: ceiling on request count in persistent batch
      device: inference device

    Returns:
      Data structure encapsulating loaded logitsprocs
    """
    min_tokens_logitproc = MinTokensLogitsProcessor(
        pin_memory=pin_memory_available, device=device)
    logit_bias_logitproc = LogitBiasLogitsProcessor(
        pin_memory=pin_memory_available, device=device)
    min_p_logitproc = MinPLogitsProcessor(
        pin_memory=pin_memory_available,
        device=device,
        # +1 for temporary swap space
        max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs + 1)
    return LogitsProcessorManager(
        non_argmax_invariant=[
            min_tokens_logitproc,
            logit_bias_logitproc,
        ],
        argmax_invariant=[min_p_logitproc],
    )

self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ):
        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
        decodes of the same sequences.
        """
        _, vocab_size = logits.shape

        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
        # have pinned memory.
        self._sampling_tensors = None

        # Initialize new sampling tensors
        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)

        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
        self._do_min_p = do_min_p

    def forward(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
        """
        Single-step scheduling:
            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
            GPU-side logprobs tensor
            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor

        Multi-step scheduling:
            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
            GPU-side logprobs tensor
            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
            tensor
            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
            in the

@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    """Tensors for sampling."""

    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor
    top_ks: torch.Tensor
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor

    @classmethod
    def from_sampling_metadata(
        cls,
        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
        output_tokens: list[array] = []
        top_ks: list[int] = []
        temperatures: list[float] = []
        top_ps: list[float] = []
        min_ps: list[float] = []
        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
        do_penalties = False
        do_top_p_top_k = False
        do_min_p = False

        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
            min_p = sampling_params.min_p

            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
                temperature = 1.0
            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
                do_top_p_top_k = True
            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
                do_min_p = True
            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
                do_penalties = True

            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
                # their logprobs
                query_len = seq_group.query_len
                assert query_len is not None
                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len

            if seq_group.do_sample:
                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens

        if do_penalties:
            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
                if (seq_group.is_prompt
                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                    prompt_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                    output_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                if seq_group.do_sample:
                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)

        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
            temperatures,
            top_ps,
            top_ks,
            min_ps,
            presence_penalties,
            frequency_penalties,
            repetition_penalties,
            prompt_tokens,
            output_tokens,
            vocab_size,
            device,
            dtype,
        )
        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)

    @classmethod
    def from_lists(
        cls,

85	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
86	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:

93	                        retain per token

96	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor

107	                                                 dim=-1)

111	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
112	
113	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
114	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
115	
116	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
117	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
118	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
119	
120	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
121	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
122	
123	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)
124	
125	    def apply_min_p(
126	        self,
127	        logits: torch.Tensor,
128	        min_p: torch.Tensor,
129	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
130	        """
131	        Filters logits using adaptive probability thresholding.
132	        """
133	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
134	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
135	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
136	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
137	                                       dim=-1,
138	                                       keepdim=True)
139	        # Reshape min_p for broadcasting
140	        adjusted_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
141	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
142	        valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_min_p
143	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing (xla friendly)
144	        logits.masked_fill_(~valid_token_mask, -float("inf"))
145	        return logits

file.content:
greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)

        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None

        # Apply temperature.
        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)

        # Apply min_p.
        if sampling_metadata.min_p is not None:
            logits = self.apply_min_p(logits, sampling_metadata.min_p)

        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
            logits,
            sampling_metadata.generators,
            sampling_metadata.top_k,
            sampling_metadata.top_p,
        )

        sampled = torch.where(sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
                              greedy_sampled, random_sampled)
        return sampled

    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)

    def gather_logprobs(
        self,
        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
        num_logprobs: int,
        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
        """
        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.

        Args:
          logits: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
                        retain per token
          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
                     with (num tokens) elements

        Returns:
          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
        """
        # Find the topK values.
        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
                                                 num_logprobs,
                                                 dim=-1)

        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)

        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)

        # Concatenate together with the topk.
        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)

        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)

        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)

    def apply_min_p(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        min_p: torch.Tensor,
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        """
        Filters logits using adaptive probability thresholding.
        """
        # Convert logits to probability distribution
        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
                                       dim=-1,
                                       keepdim=True)
        # Reshape min_p for broadcasting
        adjusted_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
        valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_min_p
        # Apply mask using boolean indexing (xla friendly)
        logits.masked_fill_(~valid_token_mask, -float("inf"))
        return logits

@classmethod
    def from_lists(
        cls,
        temperatures: list[float],
        top_ps: list[float],
        top_ks: list[int],
        min_ps: list[float],
        presence_penalties: list[float],
        frequency_penalties: list[float],
        repetition_penalties: list[float],
        prompt_tokens: list[array],
        output_tokens: list[array],
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> "SamplingTensors":
        # Note that the performance will be very bad without
        # pinned memory.
        pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()

        do_penalties = prompt_tokens or output_tokens

        if do_penalties:
            prompt_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
                prompt_tokens,
                vocab_size,
                device="cpu",
                dtype=torch.int64,
                pin_memory=pin_memory,
            )
            output_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
                output_tokens,
                vocab_size,
                device="cpu",
                dtype=torch.int64,
                pin_memory=pin_memory,
            )
        else:
            empty_tensor = torch.empty(0, device=device, dtype=torch.long)
            prompt_t = empty_tensor
            output_t = empty_tensor

        temperatures_t = torch.tensor(
            temperatures,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        top_ps_t = torch.tensor(
            top_ps,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        min_ps_t = torch.tensor(
            min_ps,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        presence_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            presence_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        frequency_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            frequency_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        repetition_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            repetition_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        top_ks_t = torch.tensor(
            top_ks,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=torch.int,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        # Because the memory is pinned, we can do non-blocking
        # transfer to device.

        return cls(
            temperatures=temperatures_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            top_ps=top_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            top_ks=top_ks_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            min_ps=min_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            presence_penalties=presence_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                       non_blocking=True),
            frequency_penalties=frequency_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                         non_blocking=True),
            repetition_penalties=repetition_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                           non_blocking=True),
            prompt_tokens=prompt_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            output_tokens=output_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
from vllm.sampling_params import SamplingParams
from vllm.triton_utils import tl, triton

_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5


def is_spec_decode_unsupported(sampling_params: SamplingParams) -> bool:
    """True if request is incompatible with speculative decoding"""
    return (sampling_params.frequency_penalty != 0.0
            or sampling_params.presence_penalty != 0.0
            or sampling_params.repetition_penalty != 1.0
            or sampling_params.min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS
            or sampling_params.logprobs is not None)


@triton.jit
def prepare_eagle_input_kernel(
    out_ptr,
    cu_query_lens_ptr,
    cu_num_tokens_ptr,
    BLOCK_SIZE: tl.constexpr,
):
    pid = tl.program_id(0)

    # [start_pos, end_pos)
    start_pos = tl.load(cu_num_tokens_ptr + pid)
    end_pos = tl.load(cu_num_tokens_ptr + pid + 1)
    num_tokens = end_pos - start_pos

118	class SamplingParams(
119	        msgspec.Struct,
120	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
121	        # required for @cached_property.
122	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
123	    """Sampling parameters for text generation.
124	
125	    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
126	    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
127	    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
131	        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
132	            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
133	            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
134	            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
135	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
136	            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
137	            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
138	            tokens.
139	        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
140	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
141	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
142	            repeat tokens.
143	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
144	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
145	            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
146	            the model to repeat tokens.
147	        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
148	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
149	            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
150	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
151	            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
152	        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
153	            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
154	        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
155	            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
156	            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
157	        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
158	        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
159	            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
160	        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
161	            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
162	            the stop tokens are special tokens.
163	        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
164	            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
165	            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
166	            can complete the sequence.
167	        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
168	            output text. Defaults to False.
169	        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
170	            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
171	        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
172	        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
173	            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
174	        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
175	            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
176	            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
177	            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
178	            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
179	            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
180	            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
181	        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
182	        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
183	        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
184	        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
185	            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
186	        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
187	            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
188	            a first argument.
189	        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
190	            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
191	            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
192	            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
193	        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
194	            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
195	        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
196	            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
197	        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
198	            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
199	            Defaults to None.
200	        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
201	            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
202	            sampling implementations.
203	    """
204	
205	    n: int = 1
206	    best_of: Optional[int] = None
207	    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
208	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
209	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
210	    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
211	    temperature: float = 1.0
212	    top_p: float = 1.0
213	    top_k: int = 0
214	    min_p: float = 0.0
215	    seed: Optional[int] = None
216	    stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
217	    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
